bugsFound.description: ParticleEmitter crash: TypeError: k.copy is not a function. Fired inside removeBlock() when attempting to create a dust particle effect on block removal. Aborted function mid-execution before isTarget check and win condition could run.
bugsFound.rootCause: C_WOOD and C_WOOD_D were Color objects created at module level BEFORE engineInit. LittleJS extends the Color class during engineInit, so pre-init Color objects lack the .copy() method that ParticleEmitter uses internally.
bugsFound.fixApplied: Removed ParticleEmitter call from removeBlock(). Game is fully playable without it. Dust particle effect can be added back during Reflexion cycle using inline new Color() objects.

bugsFound.description: Win condition never triggered even when all target blocks were removed. gameState stayed at PLAY=2 despite remaining=0.
bugsFound.rootCause: Wobble/collapse logic ran AFTER the win check was cleared by BUG-01. Once BUG-01 was fixed, a secondary issue: the win check was positioned AFTER stability recalculation, meaning if stability triggered a collapse timer, it could override the win. Fix: moved win check FIRST in removeBlock, before stability/wobble logic, with return to skip collapse on win frame.
bugsFound.fixApplied: Reordered removeBlock: (1) check isTarget + increment removedTargets, (2) check remaining === 0 → if win, set WIN state and return early, (3) only then run wobble/collapse logic.

clickCoordinates.note: LittleJS Y-up, browser chrome adds ~16px top offset in screenshots

knownIssues: Particle dust effect on block removal removed — no visual pop when block disappears. Functional gap only, no gameplay impact.
notes: Game is fully playable after 2 bug fixes. Title → Level Select → Level 1 → 3-star win all verified in real browser. Both bugs are now documented in PLAYTEST_AGENT.md bug playbook for future games.
